description: This image displays three Sentinel-1 Interferometric Wide (IW) swath sub-images, labeled IW3 (left), IW2 (middle), and IW1 (right), illustrating the spatial arrangement of radar data bursts. Each sub-image presents a grayscale view of a terrestrial area, revealing varied land cover features such as urban textures, agricultural patterns, and water bodies. Black horizontal lines segment each IW sub-image, visually delineating individual radar bursts. IW3 consists of 7 visible horizontal bursts. IW2 contains 8 visible horizontal bursts and shows a large inland water body in its upper right region. IW1 also contains 8 visible horizontal bursts and depicts a prominent coastline along its right and bottom-right edges. The image demonstrates the structure of raw satellite data, particularly the division into bursts and contiguous swaths used in Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) processing.
